items.prompt: Which open-source tool is described as lightweight for drawing bounding boxes and exporting VOC-style XML?
items.explain: LabelImg targets small object-detection box labeling with VOC-compatible export.

items.prompt: Which tool supports text, image, audio, and video with configurable templates?
choices: A model trainer without a UI
items.explain: Label Studio is multimodal with template-driven interfaces.

items.prompt: Effective annotation platforms should provide schema validation, collaboration, export snapshots, and QC review hooks.
items.explain: Those four features are the chapter checklist before production labeling.

items.prompt: List two checks from Example 4.29 to perform before scaling a LabelImg project.
items.rubric: Any two of: class names, box tightness, occluded-object handling, export format review.
items.sample_answer: Confirm consistent class names and review box tightness and occluded-object handling on a handful of exports.

items.prompt: CVAT is best matched to which need?
choices: Image and video with boxes, polygons, tracks, and team workflows
items.explain: CVAT targets larger computer-vision efforts with tracks and collaboration.
